summary: Verified ADR 0165 live on the production Windmill control plane after converging docker-runtime-lv3 and postgres-lv3 from main, applying `migrations/0016_idempotency_store.sql`, asserting `platform.idempotency_records`, and hardening the Windmill live-apply path for current runtime and control-plane behavior.

verification.observed: The limited production converge `playbooks/services/windmill.yml --limit docker-runtime-lv3,postgres-lv3` finished with `docker-runtime-lv3 : ok=146 changed=10 unreachable=0 failed=0` and `postgres-lv3 : ok=57 changed=1 unreachable=0 failed=0`.
A direct probe on `docker-runtime-lv3` returned `CE v1.662.0` from `curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/version` on 2026-03-25.
A direct probe on `docker-runtime-lv3` returned an unsealed status payload from `http://127.0.0.1:8201/v1/sys/seal-status` and HTTP `200` from `http://127.0.0.1:8201/v1/sys/health` after recovery.
A direct PostgreSQL probe on `postgres-lv3` returned `platform.idempotency_records` from `select coalesce(to_regclass('platform.idempotency_records')::text,'missing');`.

notes: OpenBao resealed during earlier production converge attempts; the successful run followed a manual unseal using the controller-local recovery material under `.local/openbao/init.json`.

Earlier in the recovery, `docker compose --file /opt/openbao/docker-compose.yml up -d --force-recreate openbao` was required on `docker-runtime-lv3` to restore the expected loopback publish on `127.0.0.1:8201`.

The repository changes in this release also hardened the live Windmill automation by retrying Docker activation checks, restricting generated PostgreSQL passwords to URI-safe characters, and retrying schedule probes that can briefly drop the HTTP connection during control-plane restarts.
